summary: FQ-15 adds a scoutable schematic map so larger worlds can be navigated, revealed on exploration rather than X-rayed. scripts/world/map_state.gd is a pure, scene-free, testable discovered-band tracker: the world is bucketed into coarse REGION(16)-tile bands, reveal_around marks the band near the player, and compact serialize/parse keep the save small (a 240x80 world has at most ~75 bands). game_root._process reveals bands around the player each frame; map_snapshot builds Town Hall and player markers plus ore and live-threat markers limited to revealed bands (the map shows only what has been scouted); map_revealed_serialized/apply_map_revealed persist the band list, wired through save_manager (map_revealed key, restored on load). _scout_reveal_radius is the scouting hook - the explorer biome_reveal perk (effect_key map_discovery_speed) widens each step's scouted band, and future exploration perks plug in the same way. scripts/ui/map_panel.gd draws a compact centered schematic from the snapshot (unseen field, revealed bands, Town Hall/player/ore/threat markers); hud builds it hidden and toggles it, and game_root refreshes it a few times a second while open. project.godot binds toggle_map to M and the controls hint gains 'M map'. Four fq15_* smoke checks cover band reveal + save round-trip, snapshot fields + real hall/player markers, discovered-band persistence through game_root, and the panel toggle plus the biome_reveal perk widening the scout radius 1 -> 2. Suite 302 -> 306. Docs fully refreshed: README (306 + map highlight + roadmap), HANDOFF, VARIABLE_MATRIX, FABLE_TASK_QUEUE. The operator's separate in-progress block-art work (scripts/art/ + generated sprites) was deliberately left unstaged.
scope: FQ-15 per docs/FABLE_TASK_QUEUE.md - a schematic map/minimap with discovered-band tracking, hall/player/ore/threat markers limited to scouted bands, persistence, and a scouting hook for exploration perks. No gameplay math change; the only save-schema addition is the compact map_revealed band list.
next_action: No strict queue head remains; recommended big-ticket playability items (pause/settings/keybinds, save-slot management, build-preview tint, quest/contracts layer on the goal system, subject/NPC labor MVP).
